figures.caption: Figure 1. Overview of the EWC learning process. After training on task t−1, model weights θt−1 are obtained. The gradient of the cross-entropy loss LCE is computed using the dataset Dt−1 with the model f(·, θt−1). These gradients estimate the weight importance matrix Ωt−1, but they are not used to update the weights. When learning a new task t, a regularization term Lreg based on Ωt−1 is added into the total loss to constrain changes and preserve important parameters in θt−1.
Table 1. EFCIL performance on CIFAR-100. Reported results represent means across three independent trials.
Table 2. EFCIL performance on ImageNet-Subset. Reported results represent means across three independent trials.
Table 3. EFCIL performance on Tiny-ImageNet. Reported results represent means across three independent trials.
Figure 5. Importance matrices of the FC layer weights (ground truth at class 2) for EWCs.
